description: Regression for INS-510/INS-203: once the user answers a clarifying question, the agent must treat that answer as authoritative and not ask for the same detail again.

conversation.text: Every morning, send my team a summary of yesterday's signups.
Where should I send the summary, and what time each morning?

text: [You have now given both the destination (Slack #growth) and the time (8am). If the agent asks again for the destination or the time you already provided — as a question or in a setup card — reply 'I already told you: Slack #growth at 8am' and do not restate the values any other way. Don't volunteer any other detail.]

processExpectations: After the user answered with the destination (Slack #growth) and the time (8am), the agent did not ask again for either detail.
The agent did not repeat the same clarifying question across turns — each distinct question was asked at most once.
The agent treated the user's answers as authoritative and proceeded to build the workflow.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: Signups source returns a few rows; a summary is posted to Slack #growth on an 8am schedule
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The signups data source returns 3 rows for yesterday: names Ana, Ben, Cara. The Slack postMessage call returns { "ok": true, "ts": "1700000000.000100" }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow is built correctly: an 8am schedule trigger feeding a step that summarizes yesterday's signups and a Slack message node posting to #growth. When it runs, it executes without errors and posts a summary mentioning the 3 signups (Ana, Ben, Cara) to Slack #growth. Only judge the workflow's structure and execution; don't judge anything about the conversation.
